observations.evidence: Embedded HTTP server banner was exposed

llm_plan.target_summary: Network-facing device with an exposed HTTP service (Boa/0.94.14rc21) on port 80.

risk_hypotheses.reason: Older, embedded HTTP servers (Boa) are known to have unpatched vulnerabilities or improper input handling, making them candidates for fuzzing.

risk_hypotheses.evidence: Embedded HTTP server banner was exposed
risk_hypotheses.reason: Lack of basic hardening (e.g., restrictive firewalls, no access controls) makes the exposed service an attractive attack vector.

test_cases.expected_signal: Unusual error response (e.g., 500 Internal Server Error) or unexpected file system response
Application crash, graceful failure, or non-standard response header
Access denied message or successful login page (indicating weak access control)

test_cases.payload: Large boundary value test (HTTP Range header)
test_cases.expected_signal: Resource exhaustion warning, excessive processing time, or standardized range error (e.g., 416)

test_cases.payload: Empty body with weird content type header
test_cases.expected_signal: Stable service behavior, or handling of unusual content types
